skill.name: prompt-engineering-patterns
skill.description: Guides users through advanced prompt engineering patterns for reliable LLM outputs, reusable templates, few-shot examples, and prompt optimization workflows.
skill.summary: Design, test, and refine production-ready prompts with reusable engineering patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly Markdown code fences, prompt examples, or benign instructional text. No prompt injection, command execution, network exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious intent was found. The remaining concern is a utility script that writes local optimization results to a caller-provided filename.

low_findings.title: Local Results Export Writes to Caller-Provided Path
low_findings.description: The optimization utility exposes export_results(filename), which opens the supplied filename for writing and serializes optimization history. The bundled main function writes optimization_results.json, and no network or secret access was found. If imported into another tool with untrusted filenames, it could overwrite a local file permitted by the runtime user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and confirmed in executable Python. Risk is limited because the included entry point uses a fixed filename and there is no evidence of malicious target paths.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, sensitive file, and path traversal patterns occur in Markdown prompt examples or placeholder text. They are not executed by the skill and do not show malicious behavior. This finding records the dismissed static clusters for reviewer traceability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed flagged locations show Markdown fences, prompt templates, and example code rather than live shell execution or malicious logic. Broad prompt-injection searches returned no suspicious override language.

content.user_title: Optimize Prompts for Reliable LLM Outputs
content.value_statement: Prompt quality often controls whether LLM applications are reliable, consistent, and easy to maintain. This skill provides patterns, templates, examples, and evaluation workflows for improving prompts in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains few-shot example selection strategies for semantic similarity, diversity, difficulty, and error coverage.
Provides reusable prompt templates for classification, extraction, generation, transformation, analysis, and question answering.
Documents chain-of-thought, self-consistency, least-to-most, and tree-of-thought prompting patterns.
Includes guidance for system prompt structure, role definition, constraints, and output formats.
Includes a Python prompt optimization script with A/B testing metrics and local result export.
Shows prompt versioning, rollback, evaluation metrics, and failure analysis workflows.
limitations: Does not connect to a live LLM provider without a user-supplied client.
Example code is educational and may need production hardening before deployment.
Some chain-of-thought guidance should be adapted for models that should not reveal hidden reasoning.
The optimizer uses simple accuracy measures and does not replace domain-specific evaluation.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Assistant Prompts
use_cases.description: Refine prompts with clearer instructions, structured outputs, examples, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Create Reusable Prompt Libraries
use_cases.description: Build consistent prompt templates for classification, extraction, summarization, SQL generation, and documentation tasks.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Prompt Changes
use_cases.description: Compare prompt variants with test cases, metrics, failure categories, and version history.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prompt Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for clarity, missing constraints, and output format issues. Prompt: {prompt}. Return three concrete improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Few-Shot Example Builder
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate design of examples for a task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create {count} few-shot examples for this task: {task}. Use consistent input and output formatting. Include one edge case.

prompt_templates.title: System Prompt Designer
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced role, constraints, and format design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a system prompt for {assistant_role}. Include scope, expertise, behavioral rules, refusal boundaries, and output format.

prompt_templates.title: Prompt Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced evaluation workflow for production prompts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an optimization plan for this prompt: {prompt}. Include test cases, metrics, expected failures, A/B variants, and rollout criteria.

output_examples.input: A support bot gives inconsistent refund answers.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a stricter system prompt, refund limit constraints, escalation rules, and test cases for common refund scenarios.

output_examples.input: A classifier confuses billing and cancellation requests.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend clearer labels, contrastive few-shot examples, edge cases, and a validation set for measuring category accuracy.

output_examples.input: A team needs reusable prompts for document processing.
output_examples.output: The skill would provide template patterns for extraction, summarization, transformation, and structured review with required variables.

best_practices: Start with simple prompts, then add examples, constraints, and verification only when needed.
Test prompt changes against representative inputs before using them in production.
Version prompts and record why each change was made.
anti_patterns: Do not add many examples that do not match the target task.
Do not rely on vague roles without clear constraints and output formats.
Do not optimize only for token count when accuracy or safety is the main requirement.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design, test, and improve prompts for reliable LLM behavior.

faq.question: Does it run prompts against Claude or Codex automatically?
faq.answer: No. The included script needs a user-supplied LLM client or adapter.

faq.question: Can it create few-shot examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains selection strategies and provides example construction patterns.

faq.question: Is this only for developers?
faq.answer: No. Product, operations, and QA teams can use the templates and evaluation workflows.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for classification, extraction, generation, analysis, and question answering.

faq.question: What should users review before production use?
faq.answer: Users should review example code, output policies, evaluation metrics, and filesystem write behavior.
